persons.arguments_against: Strongest case it is wrong: 4 Lascelles officials exist and the deterministic place check is weak (inherited). Why it fails: the forename 'Alfred G' is spelled out in both bio and staff list, the two initials A. G. agree, and the stint reproduces the bio's exact arc - Attorney-General of Ceylon (1902/1906) then Chief Justice from 1911, retired 1914 - with the staff list showing 'Sir Alfred G. Lascelles' in 1914 matching the bio's 1913 knighthood. Five edition-years co-occur. The named forename plus the AG->Chief Justice succession plus the retirement year defeat any namesake.
persons.would_overturn: A second Alfred G. Lascelles holding the Ceylon attorney-generalship/chief justiceship in these years.

notes: Full forename, AG->Chief Justice arc, retirement 1914, and the 1914 'Sir' matching the 1913 knighthood make this certain.
